title: Key uniqueness guard on capture home, not private flag
context: In unified ('shared') mode, all decision captures land in the overlay store regardless of the private:false flag. The uniqueness guard was keying its willClose lookup on the private flag instead of the actual destination (captureHome), allowing: (1) a shared-mode supersede of a public incumbent to pass the guard but then no-op the close (two live decisions on one topic), and (2) false refusal of valid overlay supersedes with private:false. Touches invariant(s): con_e04226bd05 (The MCP server must stay client-agnostic: all assistants point to the same .hunch/ graph; no Claude-only behavior in the server).
decision: Resolved captureHome before the guard. Changed willClose to key `store.decisionInStore(decision.supersedes, home === "private")` instead of `!!decision.private`. The guard now looks for incumbents in the store where this write will actually land, not where the user's flag says it should.
consequences: Shared-mode supersedes now correctly close their incumbents
No more silent two-live-decisions corruption (the guard catches cross-store supersedes)
No more false refusals of valid overlay supersedes
Error messages now correctly name the store the write lands in, not the flag
alternatives_rejected: Keep flag keying + add shared-mode special casing — would hide the root cause (destination and intent diverge) and compound guard complexity
Forbid private:false in shared mode — would strip expressiveness without fixing the architectural mismatch

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Keep flag keying + add shared-mode special casing — would hide the root cause (destination and intent diverge) and compound guard complexity
Forbid private:false in shared mode — would strip expressiveness without fixing the architectural mismatch
